description_long: This church features a multi-tiered, steeply pitched roof with a combination of dark gray and reddish-brown shingles, characteristic of Eastern European wooden ecclesiastical architecture. The structure is primarily composed of wood, with visible horizontal planks forming the walls and ornate, tiered towers at both ends. The main entrance is marked by a prominent central tower topped with a bulbous dome and a cross, while a smaller dome and spire are visible along the roofline. Windows are symmetrically placed along the sides, and the rear of the building includes a polygonal apse with a covered porch supported by slender columns. The overall silhouette is defined by its vertical emphasis and layered rooflines, giving it a distinctive, historic appearance. Decorative elements such as finials and cross motifs further enhance its religious character.
description: A wooden church with steep, multi-tiered roofs, ornate towers, and a prominent central dome topped with a cross. The structure features dark gray and reddish-brown shingles, symmetrical windows, and a polygonal apse at the rear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central tower with a bulbous dome and cross, layered rooflines, and the main entrance.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, shingled roof, symmetrical windows, and the prominent bell tower at one end.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the polygonal apse, covered porch, and the smaller dome and spire along the roofline.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the bell tower, side windows, and the extended roof over the apse.
